_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the causes and consequences of a significant event or turning point that contributed to continuity and change in a medieval or early modern society.

_source.w: We are learning about the causes and consequences of a significant event or turning point that contributed to continuity and change in a medieval or early modern society.
y: Turning points reshape societies in ways that are often felt for centuries afterwards.
Analysing causes helps us understand why events happen, while studying consequences shows their impact.
This skill helps us see connections between past turning points and the world we live in today.
eg: Investigating the causes and consequences of the Black Death on European society.
Examining a significant event in an Asian society — like the fall of a dynasty or a major war.
Exploring the turning point of a major technological development — like the printing press.
Discussing the consequences of European maritime exploration for the rest of the world.
Analysing both short-term and long-term consequences of a significant event.
Evaluating whether the event truly represented a turning point or was part of a longer trend.
